recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech has expanded its recall to include several Matrix Retail and Vision treadmill models due to a potential fire hazard. The affected models include Matrix T30, TF30, T50, TF50, T75, and Vision T9800, TF20, T40, and T80. These treadmills feature a running platform that is 60 inches long and 20 inches wide, with a console width of 33 inches. They were sold without a power cord bracket, which may lead to the power cord becoming loose from the power socket, increasing the risk of fire. The treadmills were manufactured from June 2007 to January 2024 and have specific serial number formats for identification. This recall is an extension of a previous recall involving Matrix T1 and T3 Series Commercial Treadmills, which had similar issues with power cords. The remedy for consumers now includes a free replacement power cord with a locking-style plug that does not require tools for installation.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of Matrix and Vision Treadmills Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Johnson Health Tech North America at 800-211-4590 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email retailpowercord@johnsonfit.com. More information is available online at https://matrixhomefitness.com/recalls or https://www.visionfitness.com/eng/recalls under the 'Recalls' section.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 27 reports of overheating at the power cord-socket connection. No injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech Trading, Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to loose power cord connection

Remedies.Name: Free replacement power cord with automatic locking-style plug for self-installation

recalls.metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix and Vision treadmills due to fire hazards. Free replacement power cord available. Check your model now!

recalls.Description: The recall affects the AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine, a wall-mounted device designed for full-body resistance workouts. This fitness machine features an adjustable arm and connects to the Amp Fit app for tailored training experiences. A safety issue has been identified that could lead to serious injuries. The arm may fail to lock properly, which can result in unexpected swinging and potential laceration hazards.
recalls.Title: Amp Fit Recalls AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled fitness machine immediately. For assistance, contact Amp Fit at 888-708-9560, available from Monday to Sunday, 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT. Alternatively, reach out via email at support@ampfit.com or visit http://joinamp.com/blog/product-safety-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine
Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of the arm on the fitness machine not locking properly, resulting in lacerations to the head and bruising to the shoulder and other body.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Ampfit.com from January 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $1,800

Importers.Name: Amp Fit, Inc., of Middletown, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration or serious injury due to improper locking of the arm

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair by an authorized technician at the consumer's residence

recalls.metaDescription: Amp Fit recalls the AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine due to a serious injury risk. Stop using it and contact Amp Fit for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Peloton is recalling its Original Series Bike+ exercise bikes, identified by model number PL02 and serial numbers starting with 'T'. These bikes measure approximately 4 feet in length and 2 feet in width, featuring adjustable seats and handlebars. The serial number can be found inside the front fork, behind the front fork, or behind the flywheel. For assistance in locating your Bike+ serial number, visit https://www.onepeloton.com/history-summary.
recalls.Title: Peloton Recalls Original Series Bike+ Exercise Bikes Due to Fall Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled bikes immediately and reach out to Peloton at 866-679-9129, available from 6 a.m. to 12 a.m. ET Monday through Friday. Additional support can be found online at https://support.onepeloton.com/s/article/Peloton-Recall-Information?language=en_US or by visiting www.onepeloton.com and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the page's bottom for instructions on obtaining the free seat post and installation guidance.

Products.Name: Peloton Original Series Bike+ Model PL02

Images.Caption: Recalled Peloton Original Series Bike+ Model PL02
Recalled Peloton Original Series Bike+ Seat Post

Injuries.Name: Peloton has received three reports of the seat post breaking and detaching from the bike during use, including two reports of injuries due to a fall.

Retailers.Name: Available at Peloton and Dick's Sporting Goods stores nationwide, as well as online at onepeloton.com, ebay.com, Amazon.com, and Dickssportinggoods.com from January 2020 through April 2025, priced around $2,495.

Importers.Name: Peloton Interactive Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential breakage of the seat post assembly during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers can request a free seat post for self-installation as a remedy.

recalls.metaDescription: Peloton recalls Original Series Bike+ due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair.

recalls.Description: iFIT is recalling NordicTrack Rowing Machines identified by model numbers NTRW19147.0, NTRW19147.1, NTRW19147.2, and NTRW19147.3. These models feature a black, gray, and white design with 'NordicTrack' printed in black on the front legs and 'RW900' in white on the back legs. The model number can be found on a decal attached to the rower base.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack Rowing Machines Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email ntrw19147-recall@ifit.com, or visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or www.nordictrack.com and select 'Voluntary Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: NordicTrack Rowing Machine Model NTRW19147
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 44,800 (In addition, about 700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled NordicTrack RW900 Rowing Machine

Injuries.Name: There have been eight reported incidents including two reports of fire, six reports of smoking or melting, and at least $6,000 in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nebraska Furniture, and ABT stores nationwide, as well as online at nordictrack.com and Amazon.com from November 2018 to April 2022, priced around $1,700.

Hazards.Name: Overheating screen console posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using and unplug the rowing machines; contact iFIT for a free home repair with a replacement screen console.

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack rowing machines due to fire hazards. Contact iFIT for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech Trading is recalling BowFlex Model 552 and Model 1090 Adjustable Dumbbells due to a potential impact hazard. The affected products include pairs and single units of the dumbbells, which come in black and consist of handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic tray. The Model 552 adjusts from 5 to 52.5 pounds, while the Model 1090 adjusts from 10 to 90 pounds, both using manual adjustment knobs. The model and serial numbers can be found on a sticker located on the bottom of the tray. Specific serial number ranges are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Impact Hazard Affecting 3.7 Million Units
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech Trading at 800-209-3539 from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email at recall@bowflex.com, or visit www.bowflex.com/recalls. More information is available on their website under the "Recalls" section.

Images.Caption: Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location

Injuries.Name: Johnson Health Tech Trading has received 12 reports of the plates dislodging during use with no injuries for units it sold. Nautilus received 337 reports of the plates dislodging during use for units it sold, including 111 resulting in injuries such as concussions, abrasions, broken toes or contusions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Johnson Fitness & Wellness, DICK'S Sporting Goods, Best Buy stores nationwide, and online through www.Bowflex.com and www.Amazon.com from 2004 to April 2024 by BowFlex Inc. and from May 2024 to May 2025 by Johnson Health Tech Trading Inc., priced between $200 and $800.

Importers.Name: BowFlex Inc., (formerly Nautilus Inc.), of Vancouver, Washington
Johnson Health Tech Trading Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging from the handle during use, leading to an impact hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled dumbbells and contact Johnson Health Tech Trading for a refund or replacement. Those with units sold by BowFlex Inc. can receive a prorated refund and a one-year JRNY digital fitness membership. A prepaid shipping label will be provided for returns.

recalls.metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech Trading recalls BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells due to safety hazards. Contact them for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description:
Tzumi Electronics is recalling the FitRx SmartBell XL Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbells, which range from 10 to 90 pounds. These dumbbells, identified by model number 9034 and serial number KK23289034, feature a black design with red accents and come with handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic storage tray. Users can adjust the weight by turning the handle while the dumbbell is placed in the storage tray. Both the model and serial numbers are located on the side of the storage tray, with 'FitRx' printed on the tray and handles.

The recall is prompted by a safety concern where the weight plates may detach from the dumbbell handle during use, creating a risk of impact injury. Consumers are urged to stop using these dumbbells immediately and reach out to Tzumi Electronics for a free repair that includes a new handle and storage tray.
recalls.Title: Tzumi Electronics Issues Recall for FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Tzumi Electronics at 866-363-2237 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email XLRecall@tzumi.com. Additional details can be found online at www.myfitrx.com/Recall or www.fitrxrecovery.com by clicking on 'RECALL' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells (10-90 lbs.)

Images.Caption: Recalled FitRx SmartBell XL, Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbell (10-90 lbs.), Model 9034
Location of model number and serial number on the side of the unit's storage tray

Injuries.Name: There have been more than 60 reports of weight plates dislodging during use, which have been linked to at least seven injuries, including bruises, contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.walmart.com from September 2023 to June 2024, priced between $150 and $200.

Importers.Name: Tzumi Electronics Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging during use, leading to potential impact injuries.

Remedies.Name: Free repair with a new dumbbell handle and storage tray.

recalls.metaDescription: Tzumi recalls FitRx SmartBell XL Dumbbells due to injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact for free repair.

recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech North America has issued a recall for specific Matrix-brand Training Cycles, including models CXP-03, CXC-02, CXM-03, CXV, and CXM-02. These cycles feature adjustable saddles and handlebars, an aluminum rear flywheel, and a magnetic resistance system. They are characterized by matte black steel frames, forged steel cranks, dual-sided SPD pedals, four leveling feet, and two transport wheels. The model and serial numbers can be found on a label located on the lower rear part of the cycle frame. The recalled units have serial numbers beginning with the prefixes FC32, ZFC32B, FC33, FC36, FC29D, or FC27.
recalls.Title: Matrix-Brand Training Cycles Recalled by Johnson Health Tech North America Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email cxrecall@matrixfitness.com. More information can also be found online at https://www.matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls or by visiting www.matrixfitness.com and selecting 'Recalls'. The company is actively contacting all known purchasers.

Products.Name: Matrix CXP-03 Training Cycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,885 (In addition, about 140 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXC-02
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXM-02, Model CXM-03

Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXV

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 63 reports of seats unexpectedly lowering, including two reports that users fell off the cycle when the seat lowered. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Primarily sold to commercial fitness facilities directly by JHTNA and through third-party distributors from January 2021 to October 2024, priced between $2,530 and $7,305.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. ("JHTNA"), of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to unexpected lowering of the adjustable seat during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled cycles immediately and contact Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. to arrange for a service technician to install a complimentary repair kit.

recalls.metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix-brand Training Cycles due to fall hazards. Contact for repair kit details.

recalls.Description: Precor is recalling its Resolute Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment, Model No. RMS905, which was sold to gyms across the country. This equipment features an adjustable pulley carriage designed for cable resistance exercises at various heights along a vertical rail. The brand name Precor is displayed above the vertical rail, and the model number is labeled on the underside of the pulley assembly's top frame, situated between the vertical rail and the weight stack. The dimensions of the equipment are 68 inches x 58 inches x 94 inches, and it comes in silver, black, or white frames with various upholstery options.
recalls.Title: Precor Recalls Resolute™ Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Precor Incorporated at 800-347-4404, via email at support@precor.com, or visit www.precor.com/recall. You can also find more details by clicking on 'RECALL INFORMATION' at the top of the homepage.

Products.Name: Precor Resolute Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment

Images.Caption: Recalled Resolute Cable Multi-Station

Injuries.Name: Precor has received nine reports of the pulley dropping during use, including two minor head injuries.

Retailers.Name: Sold to fitness facilities nationwide from July 2023 to October 2024, priced between $3,020 and $3,340.

Hazards.Name: Potential for impact injury due to pulley carriage drop

Remedies.Name: Free on-site repair including redesigned pop-pin and warning label installation

recalls.metaDescription: Precor recalls Resolute Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment due to safety concerns. Immediate action recommended for gym owners.

recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech North America is recalling the Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm commercial treadmills due to a potential fire hazard. These treadmills feature a running platform measuring 60 inches in length and 20 inches in width, along with a 33-inch-wide console. The treadmills are identifiable by the Matrix logo on the console masts, and the model and serial numbers can be found on the connecting bar near the power socket. The affected units were produced between March 2018 and January 2024.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm Commercial Treadmills by Johnson Health Tech North America Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech North America at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email reworks@johnsonfit.com, or visit https://matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Matrix T1xe Commercial Treadmill
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 9,700 (About 19,900 were previously recalled in January 2022)

Images.Caption: Recalled Matrix T3xe Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T3xm Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T1xe MAX Treadmill
Location on recalled treadmill of serial number
Location of model name and the serial number on the connecting bar at the bottom of the treadmill

Injuries.Name: Matrix Fitness has received one report of a fire in a fitness facility since the last recall, in addition to the 58 reported in the January 2022 recall, of the treadmill's power cord and socket sparking, smoking, and/or melting, including seven reports of fire; two resulting in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Johnson Health Tech North America and Johnson Fitness & Wellness stores across the country from March 2018 to January 2024, priced between $3,400 and $8,900.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wis.

Hazards.Name: Loose power cord from the treadmill's power socket posing a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Inspect treadmills for power cord bracket installation; ensure power cord is securely connected before use. Contact Johnson Health Tech North America for a free power cord bracket installation or to order one for self-installation.

recalls.metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm treadmills due to fire hazards. Check for safety updates and contact for remedies.

recalls.Description: iFIT is recalling the NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Voice-Controlled Adjustable Dumbbells, model NTSAW23020.0. These dumbbells, available in black, come with handles, weight plates, a tablet holder, and a base. They can be adjusted from 5 to 50 pounds in 5-pound increments using a motorized weight selector that responds to voice commands or manual adjustments via a knob on the base. The dumbbells also feature Bluetooth connectivity to sync with the iFIT app for workout guidance and data tracking.  Consumers should be aware that the weight plates may detach from the handle during use, creating a risk of impact injury.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. MT Monday through Friday, email iselect-recall@ifit.com, or visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or www.nordictrack.com and select 'Voluntary Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,400 (In addition, about 650 sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iFIT NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Voice-Controlled Adjustable Dumbbells

Injuries.Name: There were 40 reports of weight plates dislodging during use with eight resulting in injuries such as contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at NordicTrack.com, Amazon.com, and DicksSportingGoods.com from January 2022 through November 2023, priced between $500 and $600.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., f/k/a Icon Health & Fitness, of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging during use

Remedies.Name: Stop using the dumbbells and contact iFIT for a full refund. iFIT will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells due to a serious impact hazard. Contact for refund details.
